--   &gt;&gt;&gt; remove 4 (fromLinkedList [1,2,3] :: Set Int)
--   Set [1,2,3]
--   </pre>
remove :: Ord value => value -> Set value -> Set value

-- | Combine two sets into a new set containing all elements from both.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; union (fromLinkedList [1,2] :: Set Int) (fromLinkedList [2,3] :: Set Int)
--   Set [1,2,3]
--   </pre>
union :: Ord value => Set value -> Set value -> Set value

-- | Get the intersection of two sets (elements present in both).
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; intersection (fromLinkedList [1,2,3] :: Set Int) (fromLinkedList [2,3,4] :: Set Int)
--   Set [2,3]
--   </pre>
intersection :: Ord value => Set value -> Set value -> Set value

-- | Get the difference of two sets (elements in the first but not the
--   second).
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; difference (fromLinkedList [1,2,3] :: Set Int) (fromLinkedList [2,3,4] :: Set Int)
--   Set [1]
--   </pre>
difference :: Ord value => Set value -> Set value -> Set value

-- | Convert a set to a <a>LinkedList</a>. The elements will be in
--   ascending order.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; toLinkedList (fromLinkedList [3,1,2] :: Set Int)
--   [1,2,3]
--   </pre>
toLinkedList :: Set value -> LinkedList value

-- | Convert a set to an <a>Array</a>. The elements will be in ascending
--   order.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; toArray (fromLinkedList [3,1,2] :: Set Int)
--   Array [1,2,3]
--   </pre>
toArray :: Set value -> Array value

-- | Apply a function to every element in a set.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; map (\x -&gt; x * 2) (fromLinkedList [1,2,3] :: Set Int)
--   Set [2,4,6]
--   </pre>
map :: forall a b. Ord b => (a -> b) -> Set a -> Set b

-- | Keep elements that pass the test.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; takeIf Basics.isEven (fromLinkedList [1,2,3,4,5,6] :: Set Int)
--   Set [2,4,6]
--   </pre>
takeIf :: (value -> Bool) -> Set value -> Set value

-- | Drop elements that pass the test.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; dropIf Basics.isEven (fromLinkedList [1,2,3,4,5,6] :: Set Int)
--   Set [1,3,5]
--   </pre>
dropIf :: (value -> Bool) -> Set value -> Set value

-- | Reduce a set from the right.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; reduce (+) 0 (fromLinkedList [1,2,3] :: Set Int)
--   6
--   </pre>
reduce :: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> Set a -> b

-- | Reduce a set from the left.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; foldl (+) 0 (fromLinkedList [1,2,3] :: Set Int)
--   6
--   </pre>
foldl :: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> Set a -> b

-- | Helper function to unwrap a set
unwrap :: Set a -> Set a

-- | Convert a Haskell Set to a NeoHaskell Set. Only use this for
--   compatibility with legacy code. &gt;&gt;&gt; fromLegacy
--   (Data.Set.fromList [1,2,3] :: Data.Set.Set Int) Set [1,2,3]
fromLegacy :: Set value -> Set value
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => Data.Default.Internal.Default (Set.Set a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq a => GHC.Classes.Eq (Set.Set a)
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON a, GHC.Classes.Ord a) => Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON (Set.Set a)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Set.Set a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord a => GHC.Classes.Ord (Set.Set a)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Set.Set a)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON a => Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON (Set.Set a)


-- | CLI flag parsing for outbound-integration selection.
--   
--   Implements ADR-0055 §2: <tt>--integrations=fake</tt> (or
--   <tt>=hybrid</tt>) is honoured solely on the basis of the CLI flag.
--   There is no environment-variable gate. The flag is visible in
--   <tt>ps</tt>, in container orchestration UIs, and in CI logs, providing
--   a single, auditable switch.

module NeoQL.Execute

-- | Execute a NeoQL expression against a single Aeson.Value.
--   
--   Returns True if the value matches the expression, False otherwise. For
--   FieldAccess: always returns True (projection, no filtering). For
--   FieldEquals: looks up the field and compares. Non-object values return
--   False. Unknown fields return False (silent non-match).
execute :: Expr -> Value -> Bool

-- | Filter an array of Aeson.Value, keeping only those that match the
--   expression.
filterValues :: Expr -> Array Value -> Array Value


-- | MVP supports field access (.fieldName) and equality filtering
--   (.fieldName == value). See ADR-0040 for design decisions.

module NeoQL

-- | Parse a NeoQL expression from a Text string.
--   
--   Returns Err with a human-readable message on parse failure. Returns
--   Err immediately if input exceeds 500 characters.
--   
--   Examples: parse ".status" -&gt; Ok (FieldAccess (FieldName "status"))
--   parse ".status == "active"" -&gt; Ok (FieldEquals (FieldName "status")
--   (StringValue "active")) parse ".count == 42" -&gt; Ok (FieldEquals
--   (FieldName "count") (NumberValue 42)) parse "status" -&gt; Err "NeoQL
--   parse error: ..."
parse :: Text -> Result Text Expr

-- | Execute a NeoQL expression against a single Aeson.Value.
--   
--   Returns True if the value matches the expression, False otherwise. For
--   FieldAccess: always returns True (projection, no filtering). For
--   FieldEquals: looks up the field and compares. Non-object values return
--   False. Unknown fields return False (silent non-match).
execute :: Expr -> Value -> Bool

-- | Filter an array of Aeson.Value, keeping only those that match the
--   expression.
filterValues :: Expr -> Array Value -> Array Value

-- | A NeoQL expression (MVP grammar only).
--   
--   FieldAccess: bare field projection (.fieldName) — returns True for all
--   objects FieldEquals: equality filter (.fieldName == value)
data Expr
FieldAccess :: FieldName -> Expr
FieldEquals :: FieldName -> Value -> Expr

-- | A field name in a NeoQL expression (e.g. "status" from ".status").
newtype FieldName
FieldName :: Text -> FieldName

-- | A literal value in a NeoQL equality expression.
data Value
StringValue :: Text -> Value
NumberValue :: Scientific -> Value

-- | Result of executing a NeoQL expression against a single JSON value.
data FilterResult
Matches :: FilterResult
NoMatch :: FilterResult
FieldNotFound :: FilterResult


module Layout
data Blueprint annotation
data RenderOptions
data WrapMode
Balanced :: WrapMode
Fast :: WrapMode
Compact :: WrapMode
class ToBlueprint value
toBlueprint :: ToBlueprint value => value -> Blueprint annotation
empty :: Blueprint annotation
text :: Text -> Blueprint annotation
value :: ToBlueprint val => val -> Blueprint annotation
space :: Blueprint annotation
line :: Blueprint annotation
lineTight :: Blueprint annotation
softLine :: Blueprint annotation
softLineTight :: Blueprint annotation
hardLine :: Blueprint annotation
blankLine :: Blueprint annotation
append :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
appendWithSpace :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
joinWith :: Blueprint ann -> Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
joinWords :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
stack :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
joinParagraph :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
joinAdaptive :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
joinTight :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
stackTight :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
joinDenseParagraph :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
joinAdaptiveTight :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
addBetween :: Blueprint ann -> Array (Blueprint ann) -> Array (Blueprint ann)
commaSeparated :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
semicolonSeparated :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
pipeSeparated :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
wrapBetween :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
asList :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
asTuple :: Array (Blueprint ann) -> Blueprint ann
inParens :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
inBrackets :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
inBraces :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
inAngles :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
inSingleQuotes :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
inDoubleQuotes :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
trySingleLine :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
chooseWhenSingleLine :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
indent :: Int -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann

-- | indentRelative uses GhcPretty.nest (not GhcPretty.indent) because nest
--   only indents continuation lines after line breaks, while indent also
--   adds leading spaces to the first line. The test suite expects nest
--   semantics (no first-line spaces).
indentRelative :: Int -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
alignToCurrentColumn :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
hangingIndent :: Int -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
padToWidth :: Int -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
padOrBreakAt :: Int -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
mergeAdjacentBreaks :: Int -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
withAnnotation :: ann -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ann
withoutAnnotations :: Blueprint ann -> Blueprint ignored
mapAnnotations :: (ann -> newAnn) -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint newAnn
editAnnotations :: (ann -> Array newAnn) -> Blueprint ann -> Blueprint newAnn
defaultRenderOptions :: RenderOptions
withMaxWidth :: Int -> RenderOptions -> RenderOptions
withRibbonFraction :: Float -> RenderOptions -> RenderOptions
withWrapMode :: WrapMode -> RenderOptions -> RenderOptions
render :: Blueprint ann -> Text
renderWithWidth :: Int -> Blueprint ann -> Text
renderWith :: RenderOptions -> Blueprint ann -> Text
renderBalanced :: Blueprint ann -> Text
renderFast :: Blueprint ann -> Text
renderCompact :: Blueprint ann -> Text


module Json
data Value
class FromJSON a
parseJSON :: FromJSON a => Value -> Parser a
parseJSONList :: FromJSON a => Value -> Parser [a]
omittedField :: FromJSON a => Maybe a
class FromJSONKey a
class ToJSON a
toJSON :: ToJSON a => a -> Value
toEncoding :: ToJSON a => a -> Encoding
toJSONList :: ToJSON a => [a] -> Value
toEncodingList :: ToJSON a => [a] -> Encoding
omitField :: ToJSON a => a -> Bool
type Object = KeyMap Value
decodeText :: FromJSON value => Text -> Result Text value
encode :: ToJSON value => value -> Value
decode :: FromJSON value => Value -> Result Text value
encodeText :: ToJSON value => value -> Text
decodeBytes :: FromJSON value => Bytes -> Result Text value

-- | The JSON null value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Json.null  -- represents null in JSON
--   </pre>
null :: Value

-- | Parse a JSON text value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance Json.FromJSON Role where
--     parseJSON = Json.withText <a>Role</a> \text -&gt;
--       case text of
--         "admin" -&gt; Json.yield Admin
--         other -&gt; Json.fail [fmt|Unknown role: #{other}|]
--   </pre>
withText :: Text -> (Text -> Parser value) -> Value -> Parser value

-- | Parse a JSON object.
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance Json.FromJSON User where
--     parseJSON = Json.withObject <a>User</a> \obj -&gt; do
--       name &lt;- obj Json..: "name"
--       age &lt;- obj Json..: "age"
--       Json.yield User {name, age}
--   </pre>
withObject :: Text -> (Object -> Parser value) -> Value -> Parser value

-- | Extract a required field from a JSON object.
(.:) :: FromJSON value => Object -> Text -> Parser value

-- | Extract an optional field from a JSON object.
(.:?) :: FromJSON value => Object -> Text -> Parser (Maybe value)

-- | Provide a default value for an optional field.
(.!=) :: Parser (Maybe value) -> value -> Parser value

-- | Return a successfully parsed value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Json.yield User {name, age}
--   </pre>
yield :: value -> Parser value

-- | Fail parsing with an error message.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Json.fail "Invalid format"
--   </pre>
fail :: Text -> Parser value

-- | Create a JSON object from key-value pairs.
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance Json.ToJSON User where
--     toJSON user = Json.object
--       [ ("name", Json.toJSON user.name)
--       , ("age", Json.toJSON user.age)
--       ]
--   </pre>
object :: [(Text, Value)] -> Value

-- | Create a key-value pair for JSON objects.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Json.object
--     [ "name" Json..= user.name
--     , "age" Json..= user.age
--     ]
--   </pre>
(.=) :: ToJSON value => Text -> value -> (Text, Value)
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Data.Aeson.Types.Internal.Value
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON a => Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON (Array.Array a)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON a => Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON (Array.Array a)


module Path
data Path
fromText :: Text -> Maybe Path
fromLinkedList :: LinkedList Char -> Maybe Path
toLinkedList :: Path -> LinkedList Char
toText :: Path -> Text

-- | Smart text constructor to make a path from a text literal
path :: QuasiQuoter

-- | Joins paths in a cross-platform way TODO: Make this cross platform lol
joinPaths :: Array Path -> Path

-- | Append a path component to a base path Designed for pipe style:
--   basePath |&gt; append childPath
append :: Path -> Path -> Path
endsWith :: Text -> Path -> Bool
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Path.Path
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Path.Path
instance Data.String.IsString Path.Path
instance Language.Haskell.TH.Syntax.Lift Path.Path
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Path.Path
instance GHC.Base.Semigroup Path.Path
instance GHC.Show.Show Path.Path
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Path.Path


module Subprocess

-- | Options for opening a subprocess.
data OpenOptions
OpenOptions :: Text -> Array Text -> Path -> OpenOptions
[executable] :: OpenOptions -> Text
[arguments] :: OpenOptions -> Array Text
[directory] :: OpenOptions -> Path

-- | Result of running a subprocess.
data Completion
Completion :: Int -> Text -> Text -> Completion
[exitCode] :: Completion -> Int
[stdout] :: Completion -> Text
[stderr] :: Completion -> Text

-- | Stream inheritance options.
data InheritStream
InheritSTDOUT :: InheritStream
InheritSTDERR :: InheritStream
InheritBOTH :: InheritStream
InheritNONE :: InheritStream

-- | Subprocess errors.
data Error
ProcessError :: Text -> Error
TimeoutError :: Text -> Error
ToolNotFound :: Text -> Error

-- | Run a subprocess and capture output (legacy name for compatibility).
open :: Text -> Array Text -> Path -> Task Error Completion

-- | Run a subprocess with stream inheritance options.
--   
--   This is a lower-level function that allows controlling which streams
--   are captured vs inherited from the parent process.
openInherit :: Text -> Array Text -> Path -> InheritStream -> Task Error Completion

-- | Run a subprocess and capture stdout/stderr.
--   
--   This is the recommended function for most use cases:
--   
--   <pre>
--   result &lt;- Subprocess.run "pdftotext" ["-layout", "input.pdf", "-"] "."
--   case result.exitCode of
--     0 -&gt; -- success, use result.stdout
--     _ -&gt; -- error, check result.stderr
--   </pre>
run :: Text -> Array Text -> Path -> Task Error Completion

-- | Run a subprocess with a timeout (in seconds).
--   
--   Returns <a>TimeoutError</a> if the process doesn't complete within the
--   timeout.
--   
--   <pre>
--   result &lt;- Subprocess.runWithTimeout 30 "pdftotext" ["-layout", "input.pdf", "-"] "."
--   </pre>
runWithTimeout :: Int -> Text -> Array Text -> Path -> Task Error Completion

-- | Check if a tool is available in PATH.
--   
--   Returns the full path to the executable if found.
--   
--   <pre>
--   maybePath &lt;- Subprocess.which "pdftotext"
--   case maybePath of
--     Just path -&gt; -- tool is available at path
--     Nothing -&gt; -- tool not found
--   </pre>
which :: Text -> Task Error (Maybe Text)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Subprocess.Completion
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Subprocess.Error
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Subprocess.InheritStream
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Subprocess.OpenOptions
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Subprocess.Completion
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Subprocess.InheritStream
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Subprocess.OpenOptions
instance GHC.Show.Show Subprocess.Completion
instance GHC.Show.Show Subprocess.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Subprocess.InheritStream
instance GHC.Show.Show Subprocess.OpenOptions


module File
data Error
NotFound :: Error
NotWritable :: Error
NotReadable :: Error
readText :: Path -> Task Error Text
writeText :: Path -> Text -> Task Error ()
appendText :: Path -> Text -> Task Error ()
readBytes :: Path -> Task Error Bytes
writeBytes :: Path -> Bytes -> Task Error ()
exists :: Path -> Task Error Bool
rename :: Path -> Path -> Task Error ()
deleteIfExists :: Path -> Task Error ()
instance GHC.Show.Show File.Error


-- | Beginner-friendly parser combinator library.
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   One import, pipe-first style, Result-based errors:
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Parser (Parser)
--   import Parser qualified
--   
--   csvRow :: Parser (Array Text)
--   csvRow = cell |&gt; Parser.separatedBy (Parser.char ',')
--   
--   cell :: Parser Text
--   cell = do
--     chars <a>Parser.noneOfChars [',', '\n', '\r'] |</a> Parser.zeroOrMore
--     Parser.yield (chars |&gt; Text.fromArray |&gt; Text.trim)
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>megaparsec Name Translation</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   megaparsec          | nhcore Parser
--   ------------------  | ----------------------
--   many                | zeroOrMore
--   some                | oneOrMore
--   sepBy               | separatedBy
--   sepBy1              | oneOrMoreSeparatedBy
--   sepEndBy            | separatedOrTerminatedBy
--   manyTill            | collectUntil
--   someTill            | collectUntilOneOrMore
--   satisfy             | charWhere
--   label / <a>?</a>         | expecting
--   customFailure       | problem
--   try                 | backtrack
--   lookAhead           | peek
--   observing           | asResult
--   eof                 | end
--   option              | withDefault
--   lexeme              | token
--   dbg                 | debug
--   </pre>

module Parser

-- | An opaque parser for values of type <tt>value</tt>.
--   
--   Compose parsers with <a>map</a>, <a>andThen</a>, <a>choice</a>. Run
--   them with <a>run</a>, <a>runNamed</a>, or <a>runOnFile</a>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   nameParser :: Parser Text
--   nameParser = do
--     first <a>Parser.letter |</a> Parser.oneOrMore
--     Parser.yield (first |&gt; Text.fromArray)
--   </pre>
data Parser value

-- | Structured parse error returned by <a>run</a> and friends.
--   
--   Use <a>formatError</a> to render a beginner-friendly multi-line
--   message, or <a>formatErrorCompact</a> for a one-line log entry. The
--   <a>rawMessage</a> field preserves the full megaparsec output for
--   debugging.
data ParseError
ParseError :: Text -> ParsePosition -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Text -> Text -> Array Text -> Text -> ParseError

-- | One-line human description of the failure
[summary] :: ParseError -> Text

-- | Where in the input the error occurred
[position] :: ParseError -> ParsePosition

-- | Deduplicated, sorted expected tokens/labels
[expected] :: ParseError -> Array Text

-- | What was actually found (Nothing if at EOF)
[unexpected] :: ParseError -> Maybe Text

-- | Source line where the error occurred. <b>Security</b>: Contains
--   verbatim user input. Redact before writing to server logs.
[contextLine] :: ParseError -> Text

-- | Caret indicator line (e.g. <tt>" ^"</tt>)
[pointerLine] :: ParseError -> Text

-- | Heuristic suggestions for common mistakes
[hints] :: ParseError -> Array Text

-- | Full megaparsec bundle output, for debugging. <b>Security</b>: May
--   contain verbatim user input. Use <a>formatError</a> for display.
--   Redact before writing to server logs.
[rawMessage] :: ParseError -> Text

-- | Source position of a parse error.
--   
--   Useful for editor integrations (line/column highlighting) and for
--   producing precise error messages.
data ParsePosition
ParsePosition :: Text -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> ParsePosition

-- | File name or <tt>"<a>input</a>"</tt> for inline parses
[sourceName] :: ParsePosition -> Text

-- | 1-based line number
[line] :: ParsePosition -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int

-- | 1-based column number
[column] :: ParsePosition -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int

-- | 0-based character offset from start of input
[offset] :: ParsePosition -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int

-- | Error from <a>runOnFile</a>: either the file could not be read, or the
--   file was read but parsing failed.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case result of
--     Err (FileReadError fileErr) -&gt; Task.throw [fmt|File error: #{fileErr}|]
--     Err (ParseFailure parseErr) -&gt; Task.throw (Parser.formatError parseErr)
--     Ok ast -&gt; processAst ast
--   </pre>
data ParseFileError
FileReadError :: Error -> ParseFileError
ParseFailure :: ParseError -> ParseFileError

-- | Alias for the result of running a parser.
type ParseResult value = Result ParseError value
run :: Parser value -> Text -> Result ParseError value

-- | Run a parser without requiring full input consumption.
--   
--   Unlike <a>run</a>, this returns successfully whenever the inner parser
--   succeeds, regardless of remaining input. Useful for parsing prefixes
--   or streaming input.
runPartial :: Parser value -> Text -> Result ParseError value
runNamed :: Text -> Parser value -> Text -> Result ParseError value

-- | Read a file and parse its contents.
--   
--   <b>Security (F1)</b>: Reads the entire file into memory before
--   parsing. Do not call with untrusted or user-supplied paths without
--   validating file size and origin. For production use with unbounded
--   files, check size first.
--   
--   <b>Security (F4)</b>: nhcore's <tt>Path</tt> type does not sanitize
--   path traversal sequences (<tt>../</tt>). Validate paths before passing
--   user-controlled values.
runOnFile :: Parser value -> Path -> Task ParseFileError value
runMaybe :: Parser value -> Text -> Maybe value

-- | Return the current source position without consuming any input.
--   
--   Use this to capture the start position of a construct before parsing
--   it:
--   
--   <pre>
--   myParser :: Parser MyNode
--   myParser = do
--     pos &lt;- Parser.position
--     content &lt;- ...
--     Parser.yield (MyNode { position = pos, ... })
--   </pre>
position :: Parser ParsePosition
formatError :: ParseError -> Text
formatErrorCompact :: ParseError -> Text
yield :: value -> Parser value
map :: (input -> output) -> Parser input -> Parser output
apply :: Parser (input -> output) -> Parser input -> Parser output
andThen :: (input -> Parser output) -> Parser input -> Parser output
expecting :: Text -> Parser value -> Parser value
problem :: Text -> Parser value
backtrack :: Parser value -> Parser value
choice :: Array (Parser value) -> Parser value
optional :: Parser value -> Parser (Maybe value)
withDefault :: value -> Parser value -> Parser value

-- | Run a parser zero or more times, collecting results into an array.
--   
--   <b>Warning</b>: The inner parser MUST consume input on each success.
--   Parsers that can succeed without consuming input (e.g.
--   <a>optional</a>, <a>spaces</a>) will cause an infinite loop here. Use
--   <a>collectUntil</a> for termination-bounded repetition instead.
zeroOrMore :: Parser value -> Parser (Array value)
oneOrMore :: Parser value -> Parser (Array value)
exactly :: Int -> Parser value -> Parser (Array value)
between :: Parser open -> Parser close -> Parser value -> Parser value
keepLeft :: Parser trailing -> Parser value -> Parser value
keepRight :: Parser leading -> Parser value -> Parser value
pair :: Parser left -> Parser right -> Parser (left, right)
separatedBy :: Parser separator -> Parser value -> Parser (Array value)
oneOrMoreSeparatedBy :: Parser separator -> Parser value -> Parser (Array value)
separatedOrTerminatedBy :: Parser separator -> Parser value -> Parser (Array value)
collectUntil :: Parser ending -> Parser value -> Parser (Array value)
collectUntilOneOrMore :: Parser ending -> Parser value -> Parser (Array value)
peek :: Parser value -> Parser value
notFollowedBy :: Parser value -> Parser Unit

-- | On failure, call the recovery handler with the structured error and
--   continue parsing from that point.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: The <a>ParseError</a> passed to the handler will have
--   empty <a>contextLine</a> and <a>pointerLine</a> fields, since the full
--   input context is not available during recovery. For full context,
--   inspect the <a>Result</a> returned by <a>run</a>.
recover :: (ParseError -> Parser value) -> Parser value -> Parser value

-- | Capture a parse failure as a <a>Result</a> value rather than a fatal
--   error.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: The <a>ParseError</a> in <a>Err</a> will have empty
--   <a>contextLine</a> and <a>pointerLine</a> fields. For full error
--   context, use <a>run</a>.
asResult :: Parser value -> Parser (Result ParseError value)
end :: Parser Unit
debug :: Show value => Text -> Parser value -> Parser value
text :: Text -> Parser Text
textIgnoringCase :: Text -> Parser Text
char :: Char -> Parser Char
charIgnoringCase :: Char -> Parser Char
anyChar :: Parser Char
anyCharExcept :: Char -> Parser Char
charWhere :: (Char -> Bool) -> Parser Char
oneOfChars :: Array Char -> Parser Char
noneOfChars :: Array Char -> Parser Char
letter :: Parser Char
digit :: Parser Char
alphaNum :: Parser Char
upper :: Parser Char
lower :: Parser Char

-- | Parses a single literal space character <tt>' '</tt>. Returns
--   <a>Char</a>.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: Does NOT match tabs or newlines. For flexible whitespace,
--   use <a>spaces</a> (zero or more) or <a>whitespace</a> (one or more).
space :: Parser Char

-- | Consumes one or more whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines).
--   Returns <a>Unit</a>. Fails on empty input or if first character is not
--   whitespace.
--   
--   For zero-or-more behavior (never fails), use <a>spaces</a>.
whitespace :: Parser Unit
newline :: Parser Char
tab :: Parser Char
hexadecimalDigit :: Parser Char
spaces :: Parser Unit
lineComment :: Text -> Parser Unit
blockComment :: Text -> Text -> Parser Unit
blockCommentNested :: Text -> Text -> Parser Unit
token :: Parser value -> Parser value
tokenWith :: Parser Unit -> Parser value -> Parser value
symbol :: Text -> Parser Text
symbolIgnoringCase :: Text -> Parser Text
symbolWith :: Parser Unit -> Text -> Parser Text

-- | Parse an unsigned decimal integer.
--   
--   <b>Warning</b>: Internally parses as <tt>Integer</tt> and narrows via
--   <tt>fromIntegral</tt>. Values outside the <tt>Int</tt> range silently
--   overflow.
decimal :: Parser Int

-- | Parse a signed decimal integer (handles leading <tt>-</tt> sign).
--   
--   <b>Warning</b>: Internally parses as <tt>Integer</tt> and narrows via
--   <tt>fromIntegral</tt>. Values outside the <tt>Int</tt> range silently
--   overflow.
int :: Parser Int

-- | Parse a signed floating-point number.
--   
--   <b>Warning</b>: Internally parses as <tt>Double</tt> and narrows via
--   <tt>realToFrac</tt>. Precision loss may occur for values that require
--   more than 24 bits of significand.
float :: Parser Float

-- | Parse an unsigned hexadecimal integer.
--   
--   <b>Warning</b>: Internally parses as <tt>Integer</tt> and narrows via
--   <tt>fromIntegral</tt>. Values outside the <tt>Int</tt> range silently
--   overflow.
hexadecimal :: Parser Int
parenthesized :: Parser value -> Parser value
bracketed :: Parser value -> Parser value
braced :: Parser value -> Parser value


-- | NeoHaskell function syntax parsing and transpilation.
--   
--   Parsers for top-level <tt>fun</tt> and <tt>let</tt> declarations that
--   <b>capture</b> their structure as an AST for the transpiler,
--   formatter, and IDE tooling.
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Syntax.Function (TopLevelDecl (..))
--   import Syntax.Function qualified
--   import Parser qualified
--   
--   result :: Result Parser.ParseError TopLevelDecl
--   result = "fun add(x: Int, y: Int) : Int = x + y" |&gt; Parser.run Syntax.Function.topLevelDecl
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Transpilation</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   declaration |&gt; Syntax.Function.toHaskell |&gt; Layout.render
--   </pre>
--   
--   Produces valid Haskell declaration syntax:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>fun add(x: Int, y: Int) : Int = x + y</tt> → <tt>add :: Int
--   -&gt; Int -&gt; Intnadd x y = x + y</tt></li>
--   <li><tt>fun getTime() : Task<a>Time</a> { let! now =
--   Clock.now()nTask.yield(now) }</tt> → <tt>getTime :: Task TimengetTime
--   = don now &lt;- Clock.now()n Task.yield(now)</tt></li>
--   <li><tt>let pi : Float = 3.14</tt> → <tt>pi :: Floatnpi =
--   3.14</tt></li>
--   </ul>

module Syntax.Function

-- | A top-level declaration parsed from NeoHaskell source.
data TopLevelDecl
FunctionDecl :: FunctionDef -> TopLevelDecl
ConstantDecl :: ConstantDef -> TopLevelDecl

-- | A parsed <tt>fun</tt> function definition.
data FunctionDef
FunctionDef :: ParsePosition -> Text -> Array Text -> Array Parameter -> Text -> Array TypeConstraint -> FunctionBody -> FunctionDef
[position] :: FunctionDef -> ParsePosition
[name] :: FunctionDef -> Text
[typeParams] :: FunctionDef -> Array Text
[parameters] :: FunctionDef -> Array Parameter
[returnType] :: FunctionDef -> Text
[constraints] :: FunctionDef -> Array TypeConstraint
[body] :: FunctionDef -> FunctionBody

-- | A parsed top-level constant definition.
data ConstantDef
ConstantDef :: ParsePosition -> Text -> Text -> Text -> ConstantDef
[position] :: ConstantDef -> ParsePosition
[name] :: ConstantDef -> Text
[constType] :: ConstantDef -> Text
[value] :: ConstantDef -> Text

-- | A function parameter and its type annotation.
data Parameter
Parameter :: Text -> Text -> Parameter
[paramName] :: Parameter -> Text
[paramType] :: Parameter -> Text

-- | A single <tt>where</tt> constraint.
data TypeConstraint
TraitConstraint :: Text -> Text -> TypeConstraint
[variable] :: TypeConstraint -> Text
[trait] :: TypeConstraint -> Text
FieldConstraint :: Text -> Text -> Text -> TypeConstraint
[variable] :: TypeConstraint -> Text
[fieldName] :: TypeConstraint -> Text
[fieldType] :: TypeConstraint -> Text
MultiParamConstraint :: Text -> Array Text -> TypeConstraint
[traitName] :: TypeConstraint -> Text
[typeArgs] :: TypeConstraint -> Array Text

-- | A function body: expression form or brace block form.
data FunctionBody
Expression :: Text -> FunctionBody
[exprText] :: FunctionBody -> Text
Block :: Array BlockStatement -> FunctionBody
[statements] :: FunctionBody -> Array BlockStatement

-- | A statement inside a brace block.
data BlockStatement
PureBinding :: Text -> Text -> BlockStatement
[bindingName] :: BlockStatement -> Text
[bindingValue] :: BlockStatement -> Text
EffectfulBinding :: Text -> Text -> BlockStatement
[bindingName] :: BlockStatement -> Text
[bindingValue] :: BlockStatement -> Text
BareExpression :: Text -> BlockStatement
[exprText] :: BlockStatement -> Text
ReturnStatement :: Text -> BlockStatement
[exprText] :: BlockStatement -> Text

-- | Parse a top-level NeoHaskell declaration (<tt>fun</tt> or top-level
--   <tt>let</tt>).
topLevelDecl :: Parser TopLevelDecl

-- | Parse a <tt>fun</tt> function definition.
functionDef :: Parser FunctionDef

-- | Parse a top-level <tt>let</tt> constant definition.
constantDef :: Parser ConstantDef

-- | Convert a NeoHaskell top-level declaration to Haskell source as a
--   blueprint.
toHaskell :: TopLevelDecl -> Blueprint annotation
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.BlockStatement
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.ConstantDef
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.ExpressionScanState
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.FunctionBody
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.FunctionDef
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.Parameter
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.TopLevelDecl
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.TypeConstraint
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Function.TypeScanState
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.BlockStatement
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.ConstantDef
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.ExpressionScanState
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.FunctionBody
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.FunctionDef
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.Parameter
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.TopLevelDecl
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.TypeConstraint
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Function.TypeScanState
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.BlockStatement
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.ConstantDef
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.ExpressionScanState
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.FunctionBody
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.FunctionDef
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.Parameter
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.TopLevelDecl
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.TypeConstraint
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Function.TypeScanState


-- | NeoHaskell comment syntax parsing and transpilation.
--   
--   Parsers that <b>capture</b> comment content (unlike <a>lineComment</a>
--   and friends which silently skip). Use these for transpilation,
--   documentation extraction, and IDE hover providers.
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Syntax.Comment (Comment (..))
--   import Syntax.Comment qualified
--   import Parser qualified
--   
--   result :: Result ParseError Comment
--   result = "// hello world" |&gt; Parser.run Syntax.Comment.lineComment
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Transpilation</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   comment |&gt; Syntax.Comment.toHaskell |&gt; Layout.render
--   </pre>
--   
--   Produces valid Haskell comment syntax:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>// content</tt> → <tt>-- content</tt></li>
--   <li><tt>/* content */</tt> → <tt>{- content -}</tt> (nesting
--   preserved)</li>
--   <li><tt>/** content */</tt> → <tt>-- | content</tt> (Haddock)</li>
--   </ul>

module Syntax.Comment

-- | A NeoHaskell source comment, captured with its content and source
--   position.
--   
--   The three constructors correspond to the three comment syntaxes
--   defined in the NeoHaskell specification (§7):
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>LineComment</a> for <tt>// content</tt> (single-line)</li>
--   <li><a>BlockComment</a> for <tt>/* content */</tt> (block, correct
--   nesting supported)</li>
--   <li><a>DocComment</a> for <tt>/** content */</tt> (documentation,
--   Haddock target)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Obtain a <a>Comment</a> by running one of the parsers in this module:
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Syntax.Comment (Comment)
--   import Syntax.Comment qualified
--   import Parser qualified
--   
--   result :: Result ParseError Comment
--   result = "// hello world" |&gt; Parser.run Syntax.Comment.lineComment
--   </pre>
data Comment
LineComment :: ParsePosition -> Text -> Comment

-- | Start position of the <tt>//</tt> token (1-based line and column).
[position] :: Comment -> ParsePosition

-- | Text from after <tt>// </tt> to end of line (one leading space
--   stripped if present).
[content] :: Comment -> Text
BlockComment :: ParsePosition -> Text -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> Comment

-- | Start position of the <tt>//</tt> token (1-based line and column).
[position] :: Comment -> ParsePosition

-- | Text from after <tt>// </tt> to end of line (one leading space
--   stripped if present).
[content] :: Comment -> Text

-- | Maximum nesting depth observed during parsing. <tt>1</tt> = flat block
--   comment (no inner <tt>/*</tt>). <tt>2+</tt> = contains nested block
--   comments.
[depth] :: Comment -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int
DocComment :: ParsePosition -> Text -> Comment

-- | Start position of the <tt>//</tt> token (1-based line and column).
[position] :: Comment -> ParsePosition

-- | Text from after <tt>// </tt> to end of line (one leading space
--   stripped if present).
[content] :: Comment -> Text

-- | Parse a NeoHaskell line comment (<tt>// ...</tt>) and capture its
--   content.
--   
--   Consumes from <tt>//</tt> to (but not including) the newline
--   character. One leading space after <tt>//</tt> is stripped from the
--   captured content.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: This CAPTURES comment text. To skip a line comment
--   without capturing, use <tt>Parser.lineComment "//"</tt> from the
--   general-purpose <a>Parser</a> module.
--   
--   <pre>
--   "// hello world\n" |&gt; Parser.run Syntax.Comment.lineComment
--   -- Ok (LineComment { content = "hello world", ... })
--   </pre>
lineComment :: Parser Comment

-- | Parse a NeoHaskell block comment (<tt>/* ... */</tt>) and capture its
--   content.
--   
--   Supports nesting: <tt>/* outer /* inner */ outer */</tt> is valid and
--   the inner <tt>/* */</tt> markers are preserved verbatim in
--   <a>content</a>. The <a>depth</a> field records the maximum nesting
--   depth seen.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: This CAPTURES comment text. To skip a block comment, use
--   <tt>Parser.blockCommentNested "/*" "*/"</tt> from the <a>Parser</a>
--   module.
blockComment :: Parser Comment

-- | Parse a NeoHaskell doc comment (<tt>/** ... */</tt>) and capture its
--   content.
--   
--   Doc comments attach to the declaration immediately following them and
--   are extracted by documentation tooling and IDE hover providers.
--   Leading <tt>* </tt> decoration on each interior line is stripped from
--   the content.
--   
--   <pre>
--   "/** Calculates distance. */" |&gt; Parser.run Syntax.Comment.docComment
--   -- Ok (DocComment { content = "Calculates distance.", ... })
--   </pre>
docComment :: Parser Comment

-- | Parse any NeoHaskell comment (doc, block, or line — in that order).
--   
--   <a>docComment</a> must be tried before <a>blockComment</a> because
--   <tt>/**</tt> starts with <tt>/*</tt>. Both are wrapped in
--   <a>backtrack</a> so a failed attempt does not consume input.
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Syntax.Comment qualified
--   import Parser qualified
--   
--   parseComments :: Parser (Array Comment)
--   parseComments =
--     Syntax.Comment.comment |&gt; Parser.zeroOrMore
--   </pre>
comment :: Parser Comment

-- | Convert a NeoHaskell <a>Comment</a> to its Haskell equivalent.
--   
--   Transpilation rules (NeoHaskell syntax spec §7):
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>LineComment</a> <tt>// content</tt> → <tt>-- content</tt></li>
--   <li><a>BlockComment</a> <tt>/* content */</tt> → <tt>{- content
--   -}</tt> (nesting preserved)</li>
--   <li><a>DocComment</a> <tt>/** content */</tt> → <tt>-- | content</tt>
--   (Haddock format)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <b>Nesting</b>: nested <tt>/* ... */</tt> markers inside
--   <a>content</a> are replaced with <tt>{- ... -}</tt> to produce valid
--   GHC nested block comments.
--   
--   <b>Multi-line doc comments</b>: each content line becomes a separate
--   <tt>-- |</tt> line.
--   
--   <pre>
--   toHaskell (LineComment { content = "hello", ... }) |&gt; Layout.render
--     == "-- hello"
--   
--   toHaskell (BlockComment { content = " world ", depth = 1, ... }) |&gt; Layout.render
--     == "{- world -}"
--   
--   toHaskell (DocComment { content = "Calculates distance.", ... }) |&gt; Layout.render
--     == "-- | Calculates distance."
--   </pre>
toHaskell :: Comment -> Blueprint annotation
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Syntax.Comment.Comment
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Syntax.Comment.Comment
instance GHC.Show.Show Syntax.Comment.Comment


module DateTime
data DateTime
now :: Task w DateTime

-- | Add seconds to a DateTime
addSeconds :: Int64 -> DateTime -> DateTime

-- | Convert DateTime to Unix epoch seconds
toEpochSeconds :: DateTime -> Int64

-- | Create DateTime from Unix epoch seconds
fromEpochSeconds :: Int64 -> DateTime
instance GHC.Classes.Eq DateTime.DateTime
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON DateTime.DateTime
instance GHC.Generics.Generic DateTime.DateTime
instance GHC.Classes.Ord DateTime.DateTime
instance GHC.Show.Show DateTime.DateTime
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON DateTime.DateTime


module DurableChannel
data DurableChannel value
new :: forall value {w}. Task w (DurableChannel value)
read :: DurableChannel value -> Task w value
write :: Array value -> DurableChannel value -> Task w Unit

-- | Gets the last element in the channel. Note: This operation is not
--   synchronized with writes and may return stale data if called during
--   concurrent modifications.
last :: DurableChannel value -> Task w (Maybe value)
checkAndWrite :: (Array value -> Bool) -> Array value -> DurableChannel value -> Task w Bool

-- | Allows transforming and returning the array inside a lock
getAndTransform :: (Array value -> Array value) -> DurableChannel value -> Task w (Array value)

-- | Like write, but passes the index of the value to the function so that
--   the function can return the indexed value. The function f should
--   return an array of values to be written. Returns the starting index
--   where the values were written.
writeWithIndex :: (Int -> Array value) -> DurableChannel value -> Task w Int

-- | Modifies the channel array atomically
modify :: (Array value -> Array value) -> DurableChannel value -> Task w Unit


-- | Optional documentation metadata for types.
--   
--   All fields have defaults - implement only what you need.
--   
--   Usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance Documented AddItem where
--     description = "Add an item to a shopping cart"
--     examples =
--       [ AddItem { cartId = exampleUuid, productId = "SKU-123", quantity = 2 }
--       ]
--   </pre>

module Documented

-- | Optional documentation metadata for types.
--   
--   All fields have defaults - implement only what you need:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>name</a>: Human-readable name. Defaults to type name via
--   TypeName.reflect.</li>
--   <li><a>description</a>: Description of what this type represents.
--   Defaults to empty.</li>
--   <li><a>examples</a>: Example values for documentation. Defaults to
--   empty array.</li>
--   <li><a>deprecated</a>: Whether this type is deprecated. Defaults to
--   False.</li>
--   </ul>
class Inspectable value => Documented value

-- | Human-readable name. Defaults to type name via TypeName.reflect.
name :: Documented value => Text
($dmname) :: Documented value => Text

-- | Description of what this type represents. Defaults to empty string.
description :: Documented value => Text
($dmdescription) :: Documented value => Text

-- | Example values for documentation. Defaults to empty array.
examples :: Documented value => Array value
($dmexamples) :: Documented value => Array value

-- | Whether this type is deprecated. Defaults to False.
deprecated :: Documented value => Bool
($dmdeprecated) :: Documented value => Bool


module Directory
data Error
NotFound :: Error
NotWritable :: Error
NotReadable :: Error
WalkError :: Error
data CreateOptions
CreateOptions :: Path -> CreateOptions
[path] :: CreateOptions -> Path
create :: Path -> Task Error Unit
createIfMissing :: Path -> Task Error Unit
walk :: Path -> Task Error (Array Path)
copy :: Path -> Path -> Task Error Unit
getCurrent :: Task Error Path
removeRecursive :: Path -> Task Error Unit
instance GHC.Show.Show Directory.CreateOptions
instance GHC.Show.Show Directory.Error


module ConcurrentMap

-- | A concurrent map that supports high-throughput concurrent access. Uses
--   STM (Software Transactional Memory) internally for lock-free
--   operations.
data ConcurrentMap key value

-- | Create a new empty ConcurrentMap.
new :: forall key value {w}. Task w (ConcurrentMap key value)

-- | Get a value from the map by key. Returns Nothing if the key is not
--   present.
get :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (Maybe value)

-- | Set a value in the map for the given key. If the key already exists,
--   the value is updated.
set :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> value -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w Unit

-- | Atomically update a value in the map.
--   
--   The update function receives the current value (or Nothing if key
--   doesn't exist) and returns the new value to store. The entire
--   operation is atomic.
--   
--   This is safer than get-then-set patterns which can have race
--   conditions.
update :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> (Maybe value -> value) -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w Unit

-- | Atomically get an existing value or insert a new one.
--   
--   This is useful for the "get or create" pattern where you want to: 1.
--   Return the existing value if present 2. Insert and return a new value
--   if not present 3. Guarantee that only one value is ever inserted for a
--   key, even under concurrent access
--   
--   The candidate value is computed before the STM transaction. If another
--   thread inserts first, the pre-computed value is returned via the
--   second element of the tuple so the caller can clean it up.
--   
--   Returns: (actualValue, maybeDiscardedCandidate) - actualValue: The
--   value that ended up in the map (existing or newly inserted) -
--   maybeDiscardedCandidate: Just candidate when the key already existed
--   (the candidate was discarded), Nothing when the candidate was inserted
--   (we won the race)
getOrInsert :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> value -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (value, Maybe value)

-- | Atomically get an existing value, or insert a new one if the key is
--   missing OR if the existing value satisfies a replacement predicate.
--   
--   This is useful for the "get or create, but replace if stale" pattern:
--   1. If key is missing, insert candidate and return it 2. If key exists
--   and predicate returns False, return existing value 3. If key exists
--   and predicate returns True, replace with candidate and return
--   candidate
--   
--   The predicate is evaluated INSIDE the STM transaction, so the decision
--   to replace is atomic with respect to other operations.
--   
--   Returns: (actualValue, maybeDiscardedValue) - actualValue: The value
--   that ended up in the map - maybeDiscardedValue: The value that was
--   discarded (either candidate or replaced existing)
getOrInsertIf :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> value -> (value -> Bool) -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (value, Maybe value)

-- | Like <a>getOrInsertIf</a>, but the predicate can perform STM
--   operations.
--   
--   This is useful when the predicate needs to read <tt>AtomicVar</tt>
--   values inside the value being checked, allowing truly atomic
--   decisions.
--   
--   Example with AtomicVar:
--   
--   <pre>
--   let shouldReplace existing = do
--         status &lt;- AtomicVar.peekSTM existing.status
--         pure (status == Draining)
--   ConcurrentMap.getOrInsertIfM key candidate shouldReplace map
--   </pre>
getOrInsertIfM :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> value -> (value -> STM Bool) -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (value, Maybe value)

-- | Remove a key from the map. Does nothing if the key is not present.
remove :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w Unit

-- | Atomically remove a key only if its value satisfies a predicate.
--   
--   This prevents race conditions where: 1. Thread A checks a value and
--   decides to remove it 2. Thread B replaces the value with a new one 3.
--   Thread A removes the NEW value (wrong!)
--   
--   With removeIf, the check and remove happen atomically.
--   
--   Returns: Just value if removed, Nothing if key was missing or
--   predicate returned False
removeIf :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> (value -> Bool) -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (Maybe value)

-- | Like <a>removeIf</a>, but the predicate can perform STM operations.
--   
--   This is useful when the predicate needs to read <tt>AtomicVar</tt>
--   values inside the value being checked.
--   
--   Example with AtomicVar:
--   
--   <pre>
--   let shouldRemove existing = do
--         status &lt;- AtomicVar.peekSTM existing.status
--         pure (status == Draining)
--   ConcurrentMap.removeIfM key shouldRemove map
--   </pre>
removeIfM :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> (value -> STM Bool) -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (Maybe value)

-- | Check if a key exists in the map.
contains :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => key -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w Bool

-- | Remove all entries from the map.
clear :: ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w Unit

-- | Get the number of entries in the map. Note: This requires iterating
--   over the entire map.
length :: ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w Int

-- | Get all keys in the map.
keys :: ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (Array key)

-- | Get all values in the map.
values :: ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (Array value)

-- | Get all entries (key-value pairs) in the map.
--   
--   Note: This loads all entries in a single STM transaction. For maps
--   with many entries under high contention, consider using
--   <a>forEachChunked</a> instead to reduce STM transaction scope and
--   allow interleaving with other operations.
entries :: ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w (Array (key, value))

-- | Process entries in chunks to reduce STM contention.
--   
--   Unlike <a>entries</a> which loads all entries in one STM transaction,
--   this function fetches keys first, then processes entries in batches of
--   the specified size. Each batch lookup is a separate STM transaction,
--   allowing other operations to interleave and reducing "stop-the-world"
--   effects.
--   
--   This is useful for background tasks like reapers that iterate over
--   potentially large maps without blocking concurrent access for extended
--   periods.
--   
--   Note: Entries may be added or removed between batches. The processor
--   will see a consistent view within each batch, but not across the
--   entire iteration. This is acceptable for use cases like idle worker
--   cleanup where eventual consistency is sufficient.
forEachChunked :: (Hashable key, Eq key) => Int -> (key -> value -> Task w Unit) -> ConcurrentMap key value -> Task w Unit


-- | URL validation for security-sensitive HTTP requests. Enforces HTTPS
--   and blocks SSRF attack vectors.

module AsyncTask
data AsyncTask err result

-- | The winner of a <a>race</a>: <a>LeftWon</a> means the left task
--   finished first, <a>RightWon</a> means the right task finished first.
data RaceWinner a b
LeftWon :: a -> RaceWinner a b
RightWon :: b -> RaceWinner a b
run :: Show err => Task err result -> Task err (AsyncTask err result)
waitFor :: Show err => AsyncTask err result -> Task err result

-- | Wait for an async task to complete without re-throwing its exceptions.
--   
--   Unlike <a>waitFor</a> which re-throws the task's exception into the
--   calling thread, <a>waitCatch</a> returns the outcome as a
--   <a>Result</a>. This is essential when waiting on tasks that were
--   intentionally cancelled (e.g., during shutdown), where re-throwing
--   <tt>AsyncCancelled</tt> would be incorrect.
waitCatch :: Show err => AsyncTask err result -> Task err2 (Result Text result)

-- | Cancel a running async task.
--   
--   This forcefully terminates the task. Use with caution as it may leave
--   resources in an inconsistent state if the task doesn't handle
--   cancellation.
cancel :: AsyncTask err result -> Task Text Unit
sleep :: Int -> Task w Unit
process :: Show err => Task err a -> (AsyncTask err a -> Task err b) -> Task err b

-- | Run two Tasks concurrently; return the result of whichever finishes
--   first. The loser is cancelled. Errors in the winner propagate; errors
--   in the loser are ignored. Mirrors the semantics of <a>race</a> but
--   stays inside the Task error channel.
race :: Show err => Task err a -> Task err b -> Task err (RaceWinner a b)
runConcurrently :: Show err => (Task err a, Task err b) -> Task err (a, b)

-- | Execute multiple tasks in parallel, like Promise.all() in
--   JavaScript/TypeScript.
--   
--   This function takes an array of tasks and runs them all at the same
--   time (concurrently), rather than waiting for each one to finish before
--   starting the next (sequentially).
--   
--   Think of it like this: - Sequential (one after another): Total time =
--   task1 time + task2 time + task3 time - Concurrent (all at once): Total
--   time ≈ longest task time
--   
--   Note: If any task fails, this function continues executing the other
--   tasks and ignores the failure. This is useful for scenarios like
--   dispatching events to multiple listeners where one listener failing
--   shouldn't prevent others from receiving the event.
runAllIgnoringErrors :: Show err => Array (Task err a) -> Task w Unit
instance (GHC.Classes.Eq a, GHC.Classes.Eq b) => GHC.Classes.Eq (AsyncTask.RaceWinner a b)
instance (GHC.Show.Show a, GHC.Show.Show b) => GHC.Show.Show (AsyncTask.RaceWinner a b)


module Accumulator

-- | <a>Accumulator</a> is a type that allows to accumulate values in a
--   monadic way. As long as the content of the accumulator implements the
--   <tt>Combinable</tt> and <tt>HasDefault</tt> traits, it is possible to
--   use the `do` notation to accumulate values.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   ```haskell foo :: Array Int foo = Accumulator.accumulate do
--   Accumulator.push [1] Accumulator.push [2] Accumulator.push [3] ```
type Accumulator value = AccumulatorDsl value ()
data AccumulatorDsl someType result
AccumulatorDsl :: State someType result -> AccumulatorDsl someType result
[value] :: AccumulatorDsl someType result -> State someType result

-- | Pushes a value into the accumulator.
push :: Appendable value => value -> Accumulator value
accumulate :: Default value => Accumulator value -> value

-- | Updates the accumulator with a callback.
update :: (value -> value) -> Accumulator value
andThen :: (input -> AccumulatorDsl someType output) -> AccumulatorDsl someType input -> AccumulatorDsl someType output
yield :: value -> AccumulatorDsl someType value


module Map

-- | A Map from keys <tt>k</tt> to values <tt>a</tt>.
--   
--   The <a>Semigroup</a> operation for <a>Map</a> is <a>union</a>, which
--   prefers values from the left operand. If <tt>m1</tt> maps a key
--   <tt>k</tt> to a value <tt>a1</tt>, and <tt>m2</tt> maps the same key
--   to a different value <tt>a2</tt>, then their union <tt>m1 &lt;&gt;
--   m2</tt> maps <tt>k</tt> to <tt>a1</tt>.
data Map k a

-- | A builder API like this can be easily implemented through the usage of
--   the <a>Accumulator</a> module. <a>--&gt;</a> is an operator that will
--   push a tuple into the accumulator list. After that, the <a>build</a>
--   function will convert the accumulator list into a <a>Map</a>.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   ```haskell Map.build do "a" --&gt; 1 "b" --&gt; 2 "c" --&gt; 3 ```
build :: Accumulator (Map key value) -> Map key value

-- | &lt;math&gt;. The empty map.
--   
--   <pre>
--   empty      == fromList []
--   size empty == 0
--   </pre>
empty :: Map k a

-- | Creates a Map from an Array of key-value tuples.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   ```haskell Map.fromArray [("a", 1), ("b", 2), ("c", 3)] ```
fromArray :: (Eq key, Ord key) => Array (key, value) -> Map key value

-- | Accumulator operator to build a <a>Map</a>
(-->) :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> value -> Accumulator (Map key value)

-- | Set a value in a <a>Map</a>.
set :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> value -> Map key value -> Map key value

-- | Get the value from a <a>Map</a>.
get :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> Map key value -> Maybe value

-- | Reduce a <a>Map</a>.
reduce :: acc -> (key -> value -> acc -> acc) -> Map key value -> acc

-- | Merge two <a>Map</a>s.
merge :: (Eq key, Ord key) => Map key value -> Map key value -> Map key value

-- | Converts a map to an array of tuples
entries :: Map key value -> Array (key, value)

-- | Checks if a key exists in a map
contains :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> Map key value -> Bool

-- | Removes an element from the map
remove :: Ord key => key -> Map key value -> Map key value

-- | Get the value from a <a>Map</a>, defaulting to a value
getOrElse :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> value -> Map key value -> value

-- | Returns the length of the map
length :: Map key value -> Int

-- | Gets all the values in a map
values :: Map key value -> Array value

-- | Gets all the keys in a map
keys :: Map key value -> Array key

-- | Maps a function over the values in a map
mapValues :: (valueA -> valueB) -> Map key valueA -> Map key valueB


module Log
data Level
Debug :: Level
Info :: Level
Warn :: Level
Error :: Level
debug :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task w Unit
info :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task w Unit
warn :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task w Unit
critical :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task w Unit
withScope :: Array (Text, Text) -> Task err value -> Task err value
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Log.Level
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Log.Level
instance GHC.Show.Show Log.Level


-- | # Auth.OAuth2.StateToken
--   
--   HMAC-signed state tokens for OAuth2 CSRF protection.
--   
--   State tokens are used in the OAuth2 Authorization Code flow to:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Prevent CSRF attacks (cryptographically random nonce)</li>
--   <li>Prevent replay attacks (one-time use enforced by
--   TransactionStore)</li>
--   <li>Prevent mix-up attacks (provider name in payload)</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   <h1>Security Properties</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>HMAC-SHA256 signature prevents tampering</li>
--   <li>Constant-time comparison prevents timing attacks</li>
--   <li>TTL enforcement prevents stale tokens</li>
--   <li>Clock skew tolerance (60s) handles minor time drift</li>
--   <li>NO PII in token (userId stored server-side in
--   TransactionStore)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- At <i>connect</i>{provider}
--   key &lt;- getHmacKey  -- From environment/config (MUST be persistent!)
--   nonce &lt;- StateToken.generateNonce
--   let payload = StatePayload
--         { provider = "oura"
--         , nonce = nonce
--         , issuedAt = now
--         , expiresAt = now + 300  -- 5 minutes
--         }
--   stateToken &lt;- StateToken.encodeStateToken key payload
--   -- Store userId in TransactionStore alongside PKCE verifier
--   
--   -- At <i>callback</i>{provider}
--   payload &lt;- StateToken.decodeStateToken key currentTime stateToken
--   -- Verify payload.provider matches route
--   -- Retrieve userId from TransactionStore
--   </pre>

module Http.Client
data Request
Request :: Maybe Text -> Map Text Text -> Maybe Int -> Int -> !Maybe Int -> Request
[url] :: Request -> Maybe Text
[headers] :: Request -> Map Text Text

-- | Request timeout in seconds (default: 10 seconds)
[timeoutSeconds] :: Request -> Maybe Int

-- | Maximum redirects to follow (default: 0 for SSRF protection)
[maxRedirects] :: Request -> Int

-- | Maximum response body size in bytes (default: 10MB). Enforced in
--   <a>getSecure</a> only. Other methods (<a>get</a>, <a>post</a>, etc.)
--   do not check this limit. The check is post-read (full body is fetched
--   first, then size is validated). For true OOM prevention, use a
--   streaming HTTP client. Set to Nothing to disable the limit (not
--   recommended for external APIs).
[maxResponseBytes] :: Request -> !Maybe Int
request :: Request
withUrl :: Text -> Request -> Request
addHeader :: Text -> Text -> Request -> Request

-- | Set request timeout in seconds. SECURITY: Always set timeouts for
--   external requests to prevent hangs. FAILS FAST: Raises error if
--   seconds &lt;= 0 to catch misuse at call site.
withTimeout :: Int -> Request -> Request

-- | Set maximum number of redirects to follow. SECURITY: Default is 0 (no
--   redirects) to prevent SSRF bypass attacks. Only enable redirects for
--   trusted endpoints where redirect targets are known-safe. FAILS FAST:
--   Raises error if count &lt; 0 to catch misuse at call site.
withRedirects :: Int -> Request -> Request

-- | Set the maximum response body size in bytes. Default is 10MB. Use this
--   to override for specific endpoints.
withMaxResponseSize :: Int -> Request -> Request

-- | HTTP response with status code, headers, and parsed body.
--   
--   All HTTP methods return this type, allowing access to response
--   metadata.
--   
--   <pre>
--   response &lt;- Http.get request
--   if response.statusCode == 200
--     then process response.body
--     else handleError response.statusCode
--   </pre>
data Response body
Response :: Int -> Array (Text, Text) -> body -> Response body

-- | HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 201, 404)
[statusCode] :: Response body -> Int

-- | Response headers as key-value pairs
[headers] :: Response body -> Array (Text, Text)

-- | Parsed response body
[body] :: Response body -> body
get :: FromJSON response => Request -> Task Error (Response response)
getSecure :: Request -> Task Error (Response Bytes)

-- | Performs a secure GET request, enforcing HTTPS and TLS 1.2+ minimum.
--   Rejects any URL that does not start with '<a>https://'</a> with an
--   <a>InvalidUrl</a> error. The error message sanitizes the URL via
--   <a>sanitizeUrlText</a> to prevent secret leakage. Uses a TLS manager
--   pinned to TLS 1.2+ for defense-in-depth. Use <a>getRaw</a> for trusted
--   localhost callers (e.g., OAuth2 discovery).
secureTlsSupportedVersions :: [Version]
checkContentLengthHeaderLimit :: Maybe Int -> [(CI ByteString, ByteString)] -> Maybe Int

-- | Performs a POST request with JSON body.
post :: (FromJSON response, ToJSON requestBody) => Request -> requestBody -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a POST request with form-urlencoded body.
--   
--   Used for OAuth2 token endpoints which require
--   <tt>application/x-www-form-urlencoded</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/oauth/token"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.postForm
--         [ ("grant_type", "authorization_code")
--         , ("code", authCode)
--         , ("redirect_uri", redirectUri)
--         ]
--   </pre>
postForm :: FromJSON response => Request -> Array (Text, Text) -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a POST request with raw body.
--   
--   Used for XML, plain text, or other non-JSON content types.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/orders"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.postRaw "application<i>xml" "<a>order</a><a>id</a>123&lt;</i>id&gt;<a>/order</a>"
--   </pre>
postRaw :: FromJSON response => Request -> Text -> Text -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a PUT request with JSON body.
--   
--   PUT replaces the entire resource at the target URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/users/123"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.put { name = "New Name", email = "new@example.com" }
--   </pre>
put :: (FromJSON response, ToJSON requestBody) => Request -> requestBody -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a PATCH request with JSON body.
--   
--   PATCH partially updates the resource at the target URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/orders/123"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.patch { status = "shipped" }
--   </pre>
patch :: (FromJSON response, ToJSON requestBody) => Request -> requestBody -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a DELETE request.
--   
--   DELETE removes the resource at the target URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/subscriptions/123"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.delete
--   </pre>
delete :: FromJSON response => Request -> Task Error (Response response)
data Error
Error :: Text -> Error

-- | The URL does not use HTTPS. Use getSecure only with https:// URLs. The
--   URL is sanitized (query params stripped) to prevent secret leakage in
--   logs.
InvalidUrl :: Text -> Error

-- | Response body exceeded the configured size limit. The Int is the limit
--   in bytes that was exceeded.
ResponseTooLarge :: Int -> Error
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Http.Client.Request
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Http.Client.Response body)
instance GHC.Show.Show Http.Client.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Http.Client.Request
instance GHC.Show.Show body => GHC.Show.Show (Http.Client.Response body)


module Http.Client.Internal

-- | Performs a GET request without JSON decoding, returning raw Bytes.
--   Unlike <a>get</a>, this does NOT throw on non-2xx status codes because
--   it uses setRequestIgnoreStatus to disable http-conduit's default
--   status-code exception behavior. Use this when you need to inspect
--   status codes before decoding (e.g., for 401/429 handling).
--   
--   WARNING: Does NOT enforce HTTPS. For external APIs, use
--   <a>getSecure</a> instead. This function exists for trusted internal
--   callers (e.g., localhost OAuth2 discovery).
getRaw :: Request -> Task Error (Response Bytes)


module Http


-- | OAuth2 client operations for Authorization Code flow.
--   
--   This module provides the core functions needed to:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Build authorization URLs (redirect user to provider)</li>
--   <li>Exchange authorization codes for tokens</li>
--   <li>Refresh access tokens</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- 1. Generate PKCE verifier and redirect user to authorize
--   verifier &lt;- OAuth2.generateCodeVerifier
--   let challenge = OAuth2.deriveCodeChallenge verifier
--   let authUrl = OAuth2.authorizeUrlWithPkce provider clientId redirectUri scopes state challenge S256
--   -- Store verifier in session, redirect user to authUrl...
--   
--   -- 2. On callback, validate state and exchange code for tokens
--   case OAuth2.validateState expectedState returnedState of
--     Err err -&gt; -- handle CSRF attack
--     Ok () -&gt; do
--       tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeWithPkce provider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code verifier
--       -- Store tokens in SecretStore...
--   
--   -- 3. Later, refresh the token
--   newTokens &lt;- OAuth2.refreshToken provider clientId clientSecret tokens.refreshToken
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Security</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Always use PKCE (<a>authorizeUrlWithPkce</a>,
--   <a>exchangeCodeWithPkce</a>) when possible</li>
--   <li>Always validate the <a>State</a> parameter on callback using
--   <tt>validateState</tt> (CSRF protection)</li>
--   <li>Always validate redirect URIs using <tt>validateRedirectUri</tt>
--   before token exchange</li>
--   <li>Store tokens in <a>SecretStore</a>, NOT in event store</li>
--   <li>Never log full authorization URLs (they contain state
--   parameter)</li>
--   <li>Encrypt tokens at rest</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Deployment Requirements</h1>
--   
--   <b>CRITICAL</b>: Disable HTTP proxy environment variables in
--   production:
--   
--   <pre>
--   unset HTTP_PROXY HTTPS_PROXY http_proxy https_proxy
--   </pre>
--   
--   Proxies can bypass SSRF protections by routing validated URLs through
--   attacker-controlled infrastructure. Even with URL validation, a
--   malicious proxy can redirect requests to internal IPs.
--   
--   Alternatively, configure your deployment to block outbound proxy usage
--   at the network level for OAuth2 token endpoints.

module Unknown

-- | The <a>Unknown</a> type represents a dynamically typed value.
data Unknown

-- | Convert a value of any type to <a>Unknown</a>.
fromValue :: Typeable value => value -> Unknown

-- | Convert an <a>Unknown</a> value back to its original type, if
--   possible.
toValue :: Typeable value => Unknown -> Maybe value

-- | The <a>Convertible</a> type class represents types that can be
--   converted to <a>Unknown</a>.
type Convertible (value :: k) = Typeable value

-- | Applies a function that is inside the <a>Unknown</a> value.
apply :: Unknown -> Unknown -> Maybe Unknown

-- | Returns the name of the type
getTypeName :: forall {k} (a :: k). Typeable a => Text

-- | Gets the name of the type of an unknown value
getUnknownTypeName :: Unknown -> Text
instance (Unknown.Convertible a, Unknown.Convertible b) => GHC.Show.Show (a -> b)
instance GHC.Show.Show Unknown.Unknown


-- | Type-erased registry of pre-bound dispatcher closures, keyed by
--   <a>TypeRep</a>.
--   
--   Populated once at startup by <a>run</a>, read once per <tt>emit</tt>
--   at request time.

module Uuid
data Uuid
generate :: Task w Uuid
toLegacy :: Uuid -> UUID
fromLegacy :: UUID -> Uuid
toText :: Uuid -> Text

-- | Parse a UUID from its text representation. Returns Nothing if the text
--   is not a valid UUID.
fromText :: Text -> Maybe Uuid
nil :: Uuid
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Uuid.Uuid
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Uuid.Uuid
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Uuid.Uuid
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Uuid.Uuid
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Uuid.Uuid
instance GHC.Show.Show Uuid.Uuid
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Uuid.Uuid


module Schema

-- | Library-agnostic schema representation. Can be converted to OpenAPI,
--   JSON Schema, CLI args, etc.
data Schema

-- | Unit, ()
SNull :: Schema

-- | Bool
SBool :: Schema

-- | Int, Integer
SInt :: Schema

-- | Double, Float
SNumber :: Schema

-- | Text, String
SText :: Schema

-- | Array a, [a]
SArray :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Maybe a (marks field as non-required)
SOptional :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Record types
SObject :: Array FieldSchema -> Schema

-- | Sum types without fields (data Status = Active | Inactive)
SEnum :: Array Text -> Schema

-- | Sum types with fields (data Shape = Circle {radius :: Int} | ...)
SUnion :: Array (Text, Schema) -> Schema

-- | Reference for recursive types
SRef :: Text -> Schema

-- | Schema for a single field in a record type.
data FieldSchema
FieldSchema :: Text -> Schema -> Bool -> Text -> FieldSchema
[fieldName] :: FieldSchema -> Text
[fieldSchema] :: FieldSchema -> Schema
[fieldRequired] :: FieldSchema -> Bool
[fieldDescription] :: FieldSchema -> Text

-- | Typeclass for types that can be converted to a Schema.
--   
--   Types with Generic instances get ToSchema for free using the default
--   implementation.
class ToSchema value
toSchema :: ToSchema value => Schema
($dmtoSchema) :: (ToSchema value, Generic value, GToSchema (Rep value)) => Schema

-- | Generic schema derivation for GHC.Generics representations.
class GToSchema (rep :: Type -> Type)
gToSchema :: GToSchema rep => Schema

-- | Generic field schema derivation for record fields.
class GToFieldSchema (rep :: Type -> Type)
gToFieldSchema :: GToFieldSchema rep => Array FieldSchema

-- | Helper typeclass for sum type variants. Returns list of
--   (constructorName, schema) pairs.
class GToSumSchema (rep :: Type -> Type)
gToSumSchema :: GToSumSchema rep => Array (Text, Schema)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Schema.FieldSchema
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Schema.Schema
instance (Schema.GToFieldSchema left, Schema.GToFieldSchema right) => Schema.GToFieldSchema (left GHC.Generics.:*: right)
instance Schema.GToFieldSchema rep => Schema.GToFieldSchema (GHC.Generics.C1 meta rep)
instance (GHC.Generics.Selector selector, Schema.ToSchema fieldType) => Schema.GToFieldSchema (GHC.Generics.S1 selector (GHC.Generics.K1 index fieldType))
instance (GHC.Generics.Selector selector, Schema.ToSchema inner) => Schema.GToFieldSchema (GHC.Generics.S1 selector (GHC.Generics.K1 index (GHC.Maybe.Maybe inner)))
instance Schema.GToFieldSchema GHC.Generics.U1
instance (Schema.GToSumSchema left, Schema.GToSumSchema right) => Schema.GToSchema (left GHC.Generics.:+: right)
instance (Schema.GToFieldSchema rep, GHC.Generics.Constructor meta) => Schema.GToSchema (GHC.Generics.C1 meta rep)
instance Schema.GToSchema rep => Schema.GToSchema (GHC.Generics.D1 meta rep)
instance Schema.GToSchema GHC.Generics.U1
instance (Schema.GToSumSchema left, Schema.GToSumSchema right) => Schema.GToSumSchema (left GHC.Generics.:+: right)
instance (GHC.Generics.Constructor meta, Schema.GToFieldSchema rep) => Schema.GToSumSchema (GHC.Generics.C1 meta rep)
instance GHC.Show.Show Schema.FieldSchema
instance GHC.Show.Show Schema.Schema
instance Schema.ToSchema element => Schema.ToSchema (Array.Array element)
instance Schema.ToSchema GHC.Types.Bool
instance Schema.ToSchema Basics.Float
instance Schema.ToSchema Basics.Int
instance Schema.ToSchema GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance Schema.ToSchema GHC.Base.String
instance Schema.ToSchema element => Schema.ToSchema [element]
instance Schema.ToSchema inner => Schema.ToSchema (GHC.Maybe.Maybe inner)
instance Schema.ToSchema Text.Text
instance Schema.ToSchema Uuid.Uuid


module Schema.JsonSchema

-- | Convert a NeoHaskell Schema to a JSON Schema Aeson Value.
--   
--   Follows JSON Schema draft-07 semantics. See conversion rules below.
toJsonSchema :: Schema -> Value


-- | Fixed-point decimal type for precise financial calculations.
--   
--   <tt>Decimal</tt> stores values with 4 internal decimal places using
--   <tt>Int64</tt>, eliminating floating-point precision errors common
--   with <tt>Float</tt>/<tt>Double</tt>.
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Decimal (Decimal)
--   import Decimal qualified
--   
--   -- Construction
--   let price = Decimal.decimal 12.50
--   let tax = Decimal.decimal 2.625
--   let total = price + tax
--   
--   -- Formatting
--   Decimal.formatDecimal total  -- "15.1250"
--   
--   -- JSON serialization (as string)
--   Json.toJSON price  -- String "12.5000"
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Internal Representation</h2>
--   
--   Intermediate arithmetic (multiplication, division) uses
--   arbitrary-precision <tt>Integer</tt> to avoid <tt>Int64</tt> overflow,
--   while stored values remain <tt>Int64</tt>.
--   
--   Values are stored multiplied by 10,000:
--   
--   <pre>
--   12.50   -&gt; 125000
--   0.0001  -&gt; 1
--   -5.25   -&gt; -52500
--   </pre>

module Decimal

-- | Fixed-point decimal with 4 internal decimal places.
--   
--   Internal representation: value * 10000
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal 125000  -- represents 12.5000
--   Decimal 1       -- represents 0.0001
--   Decimal (-52500) -- represents -5.2500
--   </pre>
newtype Decimal
Decimal :: Int64 -> Decimal
[unDecimal] :: Decimal -> Int64

-- | Create a <tt>Decimal</tt> from a <tt>Float</tt> value.
--   
--   <b>Note:</b> Uses <a>round</a> which performs round-half-to-even
--   (banker's rounding). For most use cases (constructing from
--   human-readable literals) this is fine. For precision-sensitive
--   callers, be aware that values at the exact midpoint of the smallest
--   representable unit may round unexpectedly: e.g. <tt>decimal
--   0.00005</tt> yields <tt>Decimal 0</tt>, not <tt>Decimal 1</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.50   -- Decimal 125000
--   Decimal.decimal 0.01    -- Decimal 100
--   Decimal.decimal (-5.25) -- Decimal (-52500)
--   </pre>
decimal :: Float -> Decimal

-- | Create a <tt>Decimal</tt> from a cents value (2 decimal places).
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.fromCents 1250  -- same as Decimal.decimal 12.50
--   Decimal.fromCents 100   -- same as Decimal.decimal 1.00
--   </pre>
fromCents :: Int64 -> Decimal

-- | Convert a <tt>Decimal</tt> to cents (truncates to 2 decimal places).
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.50 |&gt; Decimal.toCents  -- 1250
--   Decimal.decimal 12.999 |&gt; Decimal.toCents -- 1299
--   </pre>
toCents :: Decimal -> Int64

-- | Convert a <tt>Decimal</tt> to a <tt>Float</tt>. This is a lossy
--   conversion and should only be used for display purposes.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.50 |&gt; Decimal.toFloat  -- 12.5
--   </pre>
toFloat :: Decimal -> Float

-- | Divide two <tt>Decimal</tt> values. Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if the
--   divisor is zero.
--   
--   NeoHaskell redefines <tt>/</tt> as <tt>Float -&gt; Float -&gt;
--   Float</tt>, so we provide this explicit division function instead of a
--   <tt>Fractional</tt> instance.
--   
--   Division truncates towards zero (integer division behavior).
--   Intermediate arithmetic uses <tt>Integer</tt> to avoid overflow.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.divide (Decimal.decimal 100.00) (Decimal.decimal 4.00) -- Just (Decimal.decimal 25.00)
--   Decimal.divide (Decimal.decimal 100.00) (Decimal.decimal 0.00) -- Nothing
--   </pre>
divide :: Decimal -> Decimal -> Maybe Decimal

-- | Format a <tt>Decimal</tt> as <tt>Text</tt> with 4 decimal places.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.50 |&gt; Decimal.formatDecimal   -- "12.5000"
--   Decimal.decimal (-5.25) |&gt; Decimal.formatDecimal  -- "-5.2500"
--   Decimal.decimal 0.00 |&gt; Decimal.formatDecimal     -- "0.0000"
--   </pre>
formatDecimal :: Decimal -> Text

-- | Parse a <tt>Text</tt> value into a <tt>Decimal</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.parseDecimal "12.50"   -- Just (Decimal.decimal 12.50)
--   Decimal.parseDecimal "-5.25"   -- Just (Decimal.decimal (-5.25))
--   Decimal.parseDecimal "abc"     -- Nothing
--   Decimal.parseDecimal ""        -- Nothing
--   </pre>
parseDecimal :: Text -> Maybe Decimal

-- | Round a <tt>Decimal</tt> to 2 decimal places.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.5678 |&gt; Decimal.roundTo2  -- Decimal with internal value 125700
--   </pre>
roundTo2 :: Decimal -> Decimal

-- | The zero value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.zero == Decimal.decimal 0.00
--   </pre>
zero :: Decimal
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Decimal.Decimal
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Num.Num Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Show.Show Decimal.Decimal
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Decimal.Decimal
instance Schema.ToSchema Decimal.Decimal


module Command
data OptionsParser value
data CommandOptions value
CommandOptions :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Version -> OptionsParser value -> CommandOptions value
[name] :: CommandOptions value -> Text
[description] :: CommandOptions value -> Text
[version] :: CommandOptions value -> Maybe Version
[decoder] :: CommandOptions value -> OptionsParser value
data PathConfig
PathConfig :: Text -> Text -> Char -> Text -> Maybe Path -> PathConfig
[help] :: PathConfig -> Text
[long] :: PathConfig -> Text
[short] :: PathConfig -> Char
[metavar] :: PathConfig -> Text
[value] :: PathConfig -> Maybe Path
data TextConfig
TextConfig :: Text -> Text -> Char -> Text -> Maybe Text -> TextConfig
[help] :: TextConfig -> Text
[long] :: TextConfig -> Text
[short] :: TextConfig -> Char
[metavar] :: TextConfig -> Text
[value] :: TextConfig -> Maybe Text
text :: TextConfig -> OptionsParser Text
path :: PathConfig -> OptionsParser Path
parseWith :: ToText value => (Text -> Result Text value) -> JsonConfig value -> OptionsParser value
json :: (Default value, ToText value, Eq value, FromJSON value) => JsonConfig value -> OptionsParser value
flag :: FlagConfig -> OptionsParser Bool
parseHandler :: CommandOptions event -> Task Error event
commands :: Array (CommandOptions value) -> OptionsParser value
map :: (a -> b) -> OptionsParser a -> OptionsParser b

-- | Generate a parser from a Schema that produces a JSON Value. This
--   allows schema-driven CLI argument parsing where each field in the
--   schema becomes a CLI option.
fromSchema :: Schema -> OptionsParser Value

-- | Generate a parser for a single field producing a (fieldName, value)
--   pair. The field name is converted to kebab-case for the CLI long
--   option.
fromFieldSchema :: FieldSchema -> OptionsParser (Text, Value)
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Command.OptionsParser
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Command.FlagConfig
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default value => Data.Default.Internal.Default (Command.JsonConfig value)
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Command.PathConfig
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Command.TextConfig
instance GHC.Base.Functor Command.OptionsParser
instance Unknown.Convertible value => GHC.Show.Show (Command.CommandOptions value)
instance GHC.Show.Show Command.Error
instance Unknown.Convertible value => GHC.Show.Show (Command.OptionsParser value)


module Decider

-- | The Decision monad for expressing command acceptance/rejection logic.
--   
--   Decision is a free monad that allows you to:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Generate UUIDs via <a>generateUuid</a></li>
--   <li>Accept commands with events via <a>acceptNew</a>,
