--   <a>acceptExisting</a>, <a>acceptAny</a>, <a>acceptAfter</a></li>
--   <li>Reject commands with a reason via <a>reject</a></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Decisions are pure and can be easily tested without side effects.
data Decision a
[Return] :: forall a. a -> Decision a
[Bind] :: forall a1 a. Decision a1 -> (a1 -> Decision a) -> Decision a
[GenUuid] :: Decision Uuid
[Accept] :: forall a. InsertionType -> Array a -> Decision a
[Reject] :: forall a. Text -> Decision a

-- | Context required to execute a Decision.
--   
--   Provides effectful capabilities like UUID generation.
data DecisionContext
DecisionContext :: Task Text Uuid -> DecisionContext
[genUuid] :: DecisionContext -> Task Text Uuid

-- | The result of running a Decision.
--   
--   Either the command is accepted with events to persist, or rejected
--   with a reason.
data CommandResult event
AcceptCommand :: InsertionType -> Array event -> CommandResult event
RejectCommand :: Text -> CommandResult event

-- | Execute a Decision within a Task context, producing a CommandResult.
--   
--   The Decision must terminate with either <a>Accept</a> or
--   <a>Reject</a>. A bare <a>Return</a> or an <a>Accept</a>/<a>Reject</a>
--   in the middle of a chain will result in an error.
runDecision :: HasCallStack => DecisionContext -> Decision a -> Task Text (CommandResult a)

-- | Generate a UUID within a Decision context.
--   
--   This allows you to generate unique identifiers when constructing
--   events.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     cartId &lt;- Decision.generateUuid
--     Decision.acceptNew [CartCreated {cartCreatedId = cartId}]
--   </pre>
generateUuid :: Decision Uuid

-- | Accept a command regardless of stream state, emitting the given
--   events.
--   
--   Use this when you don't care whether the entity exists or not. This is
--   the least safe option as it doesn't enforce any stream state
--   constraints.
--   
--   Events can be the entity's event ADT directly, or any type with an
--   <a>EventVariantOf</a> instance for that ADT.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity =
--     Decider.acceptAny [EventOccurred]
--   </pre>
acceptAny :: EventVariantOf event variant => Array variant -> Decision event

-- | Accept a command only if the stream doesn't exist yet, emitting the
--   given events.
--   
--   Use this for entity creation commands. The command will fail if the
--   entity already exists, preventing duplicate creation.
--   
--   Events can be the entity's event ADT directly, or any type with an
--   <a>EventVariantOf</a> instance for that ADT.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     case entity of
--       Just _ -&gt;
--         Decider.reject "Cart already exists!"
--       Nothing -&gt; do
--         cartId &lt;- Decider.generateUuid
--         Decider.acceptNew [CartCreated {cartCreatedId = cartId}]
--   </pre>
acceptNew :: EventVariantOf event variant => Array variant -> Decision event

-- | Accept a command only if the stream already exists, emitting the given
--   events.
--   
--   Use this for entity update commands. The command will fail if the
--   entity doesn't exist, ensuring you can't update something that hasn't
--   been created.
--   
--   Events can be the entity's event ADT directly, or any type with an
--   <a>EventVariantOf</a> instance for that ADT.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     case entity of
--       Nothing -&gt;
--         Decider.reject "Cart does not exist!"
--       Just _ -&gt;
--         Decider.acceptExisting [CartUpdated]
--   </pre>
acceptExisting :: EventVariantOf event variant => Array variant -> Decision event

-- | Accept a command only if the stream is at the specified position,
--   emitting the given events.
--   
--   Use this for optimistic concurrency control. The command will fail if
--   the stream has been modified since the position was read, preventing
--   concurrent modification conflicts.
--   
--   Events can be the entity's event ADT directly, or any type with an
--   <a>EventVariantOf</a> instance for that ADT.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     case entity of
--       Nothing -&gt;
--         Decider.reject "Cart does not exist!"
--       Just e -&gt; do
--         let expectedPosition = e.version
--         Decider.acceptAfter expectedPosition [CartUpdated]
--   </pre>
acceptAfter :: EventVariantOf event variant => StreamPosition -> Array variant -> Decision event

-- | Reject a command with a reason message.
--   
--   Use this when a command cannot be processed due to business rule
--   violations, invalid state, or other constraints.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     case entity of
--       Just _ -&gt;
--         Decision.reject "Cart already exists!"
--       Nothing -&gt; do
--         cartId &lt;- Decision.generateUuid
--         Decision.acceptNew [CartCreated {cartCreatedId = cartId}]
--   </pre>
reject :: Text -> Decision a
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Decider.Decision
instance GHC.Classes.Eq event => GHC.Classes.Eq (Decider.CommandResult event)
instance GHC.Base.Functor Decider.Decision
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Decider.CommandResult event)
instance GHC.Base.Monad Decider.Decision
instance GHC.Classes.Ord event => GHC.Classes.Ord (Decider.CommandResult event)
instance GHC.Show.Show event => GHC.Show.Show (Decider.CommandResult event)


module Schema.OpenApi

-- | Convert NeoHaskell Schema to OpenAPI Schema.
--   
--   This handles all Schema constructors and produces a corresponding
--   OpenAPI schema representation.
toOpenApiSchema :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification from endpoint schemas.
--   
--   Creates a complete OpenAPI spec with: - Info section (title, version,
--   description) - Tags for grouping endpoints (entity names for commands,
--   <a>Queries</a> for queries, <a>Authentication</a> for OAuth2,
--   <a>Files</a> for file upload) - Paths for all endpoint types -
--   Request/response schemas - Standard HTTP status codes
toOpenApiSpec :: ApiInfo -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> OpenApi


module Var
data Var value
new :: value -> Task err (Var value)
get :: Var value -> Task err value
set :: value -> Var value -> Task err Unit
increment :: Num number => Var number -> Task err Unit
decrement :: Num number => Var number -> Task err Unit


module Version

-- | A <a>Version</a> represents the version of a software entity.
--   
--   An instance of <a>Eq</a> is provided, which implements exact equality
--   modulo reordering of the tags in the <a>versionTags</a> field.
--   
--   An instance of <a>Ord</a> is also provided, which gives lexicographic
--   ordering on the <a>versionBranch</a> fields (i.e. 2.1 &gt; 2.0, 1.2.3
--   &gt; 1.2.2, etc.). This is expected to be sufficient for many uses,
--   but note that you may need to use a more specific ordering for your
--   versioning scheme. For example, some versioning schemes may include
--   pre-releases which have tags <tt>"pre1"</tt>, <tt>"pre2"</tt>, and so
--   on, and these would need to be taken into account when determining
--   ordering. In some cases, date ordering may be more appropriate, so the
--   application would have to look for <tt>date</tt> tags in the
--   <a>versionTags</a> field and compare those. The bottom line is, don't
--   always assume that <a>compare</a> and other <a>Ord</a> operations are
--   the right thing for every <a>Version</a>.
--   
--   Similarly, concrete representations of versions may differ. One
--   possible concrete representation is provided (see <a>showVersion</a>
--   and <a>parseVersion</a>), but depending on the application a different
--   concrete representation may be more appropriate.
data Version

-- | Smart text constructor for versions.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   let ver = [version|0.0.0-alpha|]
--   </pre>
--   
--   This will create a version with branch [0, 0, 0] and tags ["alpha"].
version :: QuasiQuoter

-- | Parse a version from a Text.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Version.parse "0.0.0-alpha"
--   </pre>
--   
--   Just (Version [0, 0, 0] ["alpha"])
parse :: Text -> Maybe Version
toText :: Version -> Text
instance Language.Haskell.TH.Syntax.Lift Data.Version.Version


-- | # Core
--   
--   This module is automatically imported in all the NeoHaskell files,

module Core

-- | Smart text constructor to make a path from a text literal
path :: QuasiQuoter

-- | Representation of fast immutable arrays. You can create arrays of
--   integers (<tt>Array Int</tt>) or strings (<tt>Array String</tt>) or
--   any other type of value you can dream up.
data Array a
data Bytes

-- | A channel for communication between concurrent tasks.
--   
--   Channels can be either: - **Unbounded**: Writers never block, but
--   memory can grow without limit - **Bounded**: Writers block when
--   channel is full, providing backpressure
--   
--   Use unbounded channels for low-volume or when backpressure isn't
--   needed. Use bounded channels for high-throughput scenarios to prevent
--   OOM.
--   
--   <h2>Performance Notes</h2>
--   
--   Bounded channels use STM's TBQueue for correctness guarantees. Under
--   extreme backpressure (channel consistently full with many concurrent
--   writers), STM transaction retries can cause throughput degradation
--   ("STM thrash").
--   
--   For most workloads (&lt; 50k events/second), this is not a concern. If
--   you observe throughput collapse under sustained backpressure,
--   consider:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Increasing channel capacity to reduce full-channel frequency</li>
--   <li>Adding more workers (horizontal scaling)</li>
--   <li>Investigating why consumers are slower than producers</li>
--   </ol>
data Channel value

-- | The character type <a>Char</a> represents Unicode codespace and its
--   elements are code points as in definitions <a>D9 and D10 of the
--   Unicode Standard</a>.
--   
--   Character literals in Haskell are single-quoted: <tt>'Q'</tt>,
--   <tt>'Я'</tt> or <tt>'Ω'</tt>. To represent a single quote itself use
--   <tt>'\''</tt>, and to represent a backslash use <tt>'\\'</tt>. The
--   full grammar can be found in the section 2.6 of the <a>Haskell 2010
--   Language Report</a>.
--   
--   To specify a character by its code point one can use decimal,
--   hexadecimal or octal notation: <tt>'\65'</tt>, <tt>'\x41'</tt> and
--   <tt>'\o101'</tt> are all alternative forms of <tt>'A'</tt>. The
--   largest code point is <tt>'\x10ffff'</tt>.
--   
--   There is a special escape syntax for ASCII control characters:
--   
--   TODO: table
--   
--   <a>Data.Char</a> provides utilities to work with <a>Char</a>.
data Char

-- | The Command typeclass defines the contract for all commands.
--   
--   Commands represent user intent to change the system. Each command must
--   provide:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>getEntityIdImpl</a>: Extract the entity ID from the command
--   (Nothing for creation)</li>
--   <li><a>decideImpl</a>: Pure decision logic that produces events or
--   rejects</li>
--   </ul>
class Command command where {
    
    -- | Whether this command supports multi-tenancy.
    --   
    --   When True, <a>getEntityIdImpl</a> and <a>decideImpl</a> receive a
    --   tenant UUID as first argument.
    type IsMultiTenant command :: Bool;
    type IsMultiTenant command = 'False;
}

-- | Extract the entity ID from a command.
--   
--   Returns Nothing for creation commands where no entity exists yet.
getEntityIdImpl :: Command command => GetEntityIdFunction (IsMultiTenant command) command (EntityIdType (EntityOf command))

-- | The decision function that determines whether to accept or reject the
--   command.
--   
--   This is pure business logic that takes the command and optional
--   current entity state, returning a Decision that either accepts with
--   events or rejects with a reason.
decideImpl :: Command command => DecideFunction (IsMultiTenant command) command (EntityOf command) (EventOf (EntityOf command))

-- | Authorization check run before <a>decideImpl</a>.
--   
--   Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if the caller may execute this command, or
--   <tt>Just</tt> an <a>AccessError</a> explaining why not. Defaults to
--   <a>authenticatedAccess</a> so a command with no <tt>canAccess</tt>
--   definition blocks unauthenticated callers — going public is an
--   explicit opt-in.
--   
--   The name pairs with the dispatcher entry point (<tt>execute</tt>) and
--   avoids a re-export clash with <a>canAccessImpl</a>. The user still
--   writes a top-level <tt>canAccess</tt>; the TH macro wires it here.
--   
--   An invalid token is never silently accepted: the Web transport rejects
--   any AuthError other than TokenMissing before reaching this method.
canExecuteImpl :: Command command => Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError
data ConcurrentVar value
data DateTime

-- | Fixed-point decimal with 4 internal decimal places.
--   
--   Internal representation: value * 10000
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal 125000  -- represents 12.5000
--   Decimal 1       -- represents 0.0001
--   Decimal (-52500) -- represents -5.2500
--   </pre>
data Decimal
data DurableChannel value

-- | A value of type <tt><a>IO</a> a</tt> is a computation which, when
--   performed, does some I/O before returning a value of type <tt>a</tt>.
--   
--   There is really only one way to "perform" an I/O action: bind it to
--   <tt>Main.main</tt> in your program. When your program is run, the I/O
--   will be performed. It isn't possible to perform I/O from an arbitrary
--   function, unless that function is itself in the <a>IO</a> monad and
--   called at some point, directly or indirectly, from <tt>Main.main</tt>.
--   
--   <a>IO</a> is a monad, so <a>IO</a> actions can be combined using
--   either the do-notation or the <a>&gt;&gt;</a> and <a>&gt;&gt;=</a>
--   operations from the <a>Monad</a> class.
data IO a

-- | In Haskell a list type is defined using square brackets. This alias
--   allows us to alternatively write the type like we would in Elm.
type LinkedList a = [a]
data Lock

-- | A Map from keys <tt>k</tt> to values <tt>a</tt>.
--   
--   The <a>Semigroup</a> operation for <a>Map</a> is <a>union</a>, which
--   prefers values from the left operand. If <tt>m1</tt> maps a key
--   <tt>k</tt> to a value <tt>a1</tt>, and <tt>m2</tt> maps the same key
--   to a different value <tt>a2</tt>, then their union <tt>m1 &lt;&gt;
--   m2</tt> maps <tt>k</tt> to <tt>a1</tt>.
data Map k a

-- | The <a>Maybe</a> type encapsulates an optional value. A value of type
--   <tt><a>Maybe</a> a</tt> either contains a value of type <tt>a</tt>
--   (represented as <tt><a>Just</a> a</tt>), or it is empty (represented
--   as <a>Nothing</a>). Using <a>Maybe</a> is a good way to deal with
--   errors or exceptional cases without resorting to drastic measures such
--   as <a>error</a>.
--   
--   The <a>Maybe</a> type is also a monad. It is a simple kind of error
--   monad, where all errors are represented by <a>Nothing</a>. A richer
--   error monad can be built using the <a>Either</a> type.
data Maybe a
Nothing :: Maybe a
Just :: a -> Maybe a
data Path
type Record (r :: Row Type) = Record I r

-- | A value that shows as <tt>&lt;redacted&gt;</tt> in Show output.
--   
--   Two constructors:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>Redacted</a> value: Shows as <tt>&lt;redacted&gt;</tt> — most
--   common case</li>
--   <li><a>RedactedLabeled</a> label value: Shows as <tt>&lt;redacted:
--   label&gt;</tt> — for debugging complex structures</li>
--   </ul>
data Redacted value

-- | A <tt>Result</tt> is either <tt>Ok</tt> meaning the computation
--   succeeded, or it is an <tt>Err</tt> meaning that there was some
--   failure.
data Result error value
Ok :: value -> Result error value
Err :: error -> Result error value

-- | Library-agnostic schema representation. Can be converted to OpenAPI,
--   JSON Schema, CLI args, etc.
data Schema

-- | Unit, ()
SNull :: Schema

-- | Bool
SBool :: Schema

-- | Int, Integer
SInt :: Schema

-- | Double, Float
SNumber :: Schema

-- | Text, String
SText :: Schema

-- | Array a, [a]
SArray :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Maybe a (marks field as non-required)
SOptional :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Record types
SObject :: Array FieldSchema -> Schema

-- | Sum types without fields (data Status = Active | Inactive)
SEnum :: Array Text -> Schema

-- | Sum types with fields (data Shape = Circle {radius :: Int} | ...)
SUnion :: Array (Text, Schema) -> Schema

-- | Reference for recursive types
SRef :: Text -> Schema
data Service (commandRow :: Row Type) (commandTransportNames :: [Symbol])
data Task err value

-- | A <tt>Text</tt> is a chunk of text:
--   
--   <pre>
--   "Hello!"
--   "How are you?"
--   "🙈🙉🙊"
--   
--   -- strings with escape characters
--   "this\n\t\"that\""
--   "\x1F648\x1F649\x1F64A" -- "🙈🙉🙊"
--   </pre>
--   
--   A <tt>Text</tt> can represent any sequence of <a>unicode
--   characters</a>. You can use the unicode escapes from <tt>x0000</tt> to
--   <tt>x10FFFF</tt> to represent characters by their code point. You can
--   also include the unicode characters directly. Using the escapes can be
--   better if you need one of the many whitespace characters with
--   different widths.
type Text = Text

-- | A trigger is a way to listen to a process that's running in the
--   background and generates events. When you tell NeoHaskell to subscribe
--   to a process, NeoHaskell will pass a callback function that posts an
--   event to the event queue whenever the process generates an event. The
--   callback function is called a <tt>dispatch</tt> function.
--   
--   An example Trigger is <a>every</a>, which generates an event every
--   specified milliseconds. When you subscribe to <a>every</a>, you pass a
--   callback function that posts an event to the event queue whenever the
--   process ticks.
data Trigger event

-- | The <a>Unknown</a> type represents a dynamically typed value.
data Unknown
data Uuid
data Var value

-- | A <a>Version</a> represents the version of a software entity.
--   
--   An instance of <a>Eq</a> is provided, which implements exact equality
--   modulo reordering of the tags in the <a>versionTags</a> field.
--   
--   An instance of <a>Ord</a> is also provided, which gives lexicographic
--   ordering on the <a>versionBranch</a> fields (i.e. 2.1 &gt; 2.0, 1.2.3
--   &gt; 1.2.2, etc.). This is expected to be sufficient for many uses,
--   but note that you may need to use a more specific ordering for your
--   versioning scheme. For example, some versioning schemes may include
--   pre-releases which have tags <tt>"pre1"</tt>, <tt>"pre2"</tt>, and so
--   on, and these would need to be taken into account when determining
--   ordering. In some cases, date ordering may be more appropriate, so the
--   application would have to look for <tt>date</tt> tags in the
--   <a>versionTags</a> field and compare those. The bottom line is, don't
--   always assume that <a>compare</a> and other <a>Ord</a> operations are
--   the right thing for every <a>Version</a>.
--   
--   Similarly, concrete representations of versions may differ. One
--   possible concrete representation is provided (see <a>showVersion</a>
--   and <a>parseVersion</a>), but depending on the application a different
--   concrete representation may be more appropriate.
data Version

-- | Put two appendable things together. This includes arrays, lists, and
--   text.
--   
--   <pre>
--   "hello" ++ "world" == "helloworld"
--   [1,1,2] ++ [3,5,8] == [1,1,2,3,5,8]
--   </pre>
(++) :: Appendable appendable => appendable -> appendable -> appendable
infixr 5 ++

-- | The class <a>Typeable</a> allows a concrete representation of a type
--   to be calculated.
class Typeable (a :: k)

-- | Smart text constructor for versions.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   let ver = [version|0.0.0-alpha|]
--   </pre>
--   
--   This will create a version with branch [0, 0, 0] and tags ["alpha"].
version :: QuasiQuoter

-- | Schema for a single field in a record type.
data FieldSchema
FieldSchema :: Text -> Schema -> Bool -> Text -> FieldSchema
[fieldName] :: FieldSchema -> Text
[fieldSchema] :: FieldSchema -> Schema
[fieldRequired] :: FieldSchema -> Bool
[fieldDescription] :: FieldSchema -> Text

-- | The result of running a Decision.
--   
--   Either the command is accepted with events to persist, or rejected
--   with a reason.
data CommandResult event
AcceptCommand :: InsertionType -> Array event -> CommandResult event
RejectCommand :: Text -> CommandResult event

-- | The Decision monad for expressing command acceptance/rejection logic.
--   
--   Decision is a free monad that allows you to:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Generate UUIDs via <a>generateUuid</a></li>
--   <li>Accept commands with events via <a>acceptNew</a>,
--   <a>acceptExisting</a>, <a>acceptAny</a>, <a>acceptAfter</a></li>
--   <li>Reject commands with a reason via <a>reject</a></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Decisions are pure and can be easily tested without side effects.
data Decision a
class KnownHash (s :: Symbol)
hashVal :: KnownHash s => proxy s -> Int

-- | Typeclass for types that can be converted to a Schema.
--   
--   Types with Generic instances get ToSchema for free using the default
--   implementation.
class ToSchema value
toSchema :: ToSchema value => Schema
($dmtoSchema) :: (ToSchema value, Generic value, GToSchema (Rep value)) => Schema

-- | Whether this command supports multi-tenancy.
--   
--   When True, <a>getEntityIdImpl</a> and <a>decideImpl</a> receive a
--   tenant UUID as first argument.
type family IsMultiTenant command :: Bool

-- | Maps a type to its symbolic name.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance NameOf CreateCart = <a>CreateCart</a>
--   </pre>
type family NameOf t :: Symbol

-- | The Entity typeclass represents domain objects that can be
--   reconstructed from events.
--   
--   Entities are the "left fold" of their event history - starting from an
--   initial state and applying each event in sequence via
--   <a>updateImpl</a>.
class Entity entity where {
    
    -- | The type of identifier used for this entity.
    --   
    --   Defaults to <a>Uuid</a> but can be overridden for entities that use
    --   different ID types.
    type EntityIdType entity;
    type EntityIdType entity = Uuid;
}

-- | The initial state of the entity before any events are applied.
--   
--   This is used as the starting point when reconstructing an entity from
--   its event stream.
initialStateImpl :: Entity entity => entity

-- | Apply an event to the entity state, producing a new state.
--   
--   This is the reducer function used to reconstruct entity state from
--   events. It should be a pure function with no side effects.
updateImpl :: Entity entity => EventOf entity -> entity -> entity

-- | Maps a command type to its corresponding entity type.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance EntityOf CreateCart = CartEntity
--   </pre>
type family EntityOf command

-- | Maps an entity type to its corresponding event type.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance EventOf CartEntity = CartEvent
--   </pre>
type family EventOf entityType
data InsertionType
StreamCreation :: InsertionType
InsertAfter :: StreamPosition -> InsertionType
ExistingStream :: InsertionType
AnyStreamState :: InsertionType
data StreamId

-- | Typeclass for types that can be converted to StreamId.
--   
--   Note: These conversions are considered "safe" because: - StreamId →
--   StreamId: Already validated - Uuid → StreamId: UUIDs are fixed-length
--   (36 chars), always valid
--   
--   For Text, use <a>fromText</a> which validates length.
class ToStreamId idType
toStreamId :: ToStreamId idType => idType -> StreamId

-- | The type of identifier used for this entity.
--   
--   Defaults to <a>Uuid</a> but can be overridden for entities that use
--   different ID types.
type family EntityIdType entity
